text: The missional potential in Ezra-Nehemiah This article focuses on the missional potential in the biblical books of Ezra and Nehemiah seen as a single, two-volume literary work. As we have seen, the written Word of God can impact society in transformation from a missionary perspective only through the mediation and witness of the church, an awareness that points to the spiritual renewal needed for any lasting social rebuilding. The study will focus on a hermeneutical level on a historical, canonical and exegetical analysis of the scriptural function behind the transition from oral to literate culture and with this basis draw up some missional conclusions to further develop a contemporary, relevant theology of mission.
